title: Bootstrap CSS Conflicts
text: embedding muiDataGrid inside Shiny app, may notice grid looks visually : fonts appear small, spacing feels wrong, components render expected size. almost always caused CSS conflict Bootstrap MUI.

title: Bootstrap CSS Conflicts
text: Default R Shiny uses Bootstrap 3, example sets global font-size: 10px page. MUI components rely browser’s default font size sizing, Bootstrap rule causes grid render small — compressed rows, small icons, broken spacing.

previous_headings: Bootstrap CSS Conflicts > Approaches to Resolve the Conflict
title: Bootstrap CSS Conflicts
text: cleanest solution build entire Shiny UI using muiMaterial R package. muiMaterial wraps MUI components use Shiny, Bootstrap picture . MUI’s CSS baseline applies correctly, muiDataGrid renders intended. approach reliable muiDataGrid surrounding UI share CSS foundation.
code: library(shiny) library(muiMaterial) library(muiDataGrid)  ui <- muiMaterial::muiMaterialPage(   muiMaterial::Typography("R Shiny with muiMaterial and muiDataGrid"),   muiDataGrid::DataGrid(     rows = head(dplyr::select(dplyr::starwars, name, height, mass))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {}  shinyApp(ui, server)

previous_headings: Bootstrap CSS Conflicts > Approaches to Resolve the Conflict
title: Bootstrap CSS Conflicts
text: Bootstrap 5 removed html { font-size: 10px } rule reverts browser default 16px. want keep Bootstrap app, switching bslib Bootstrap 5 eliminates conflict.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(muiDataGrid)  ui <- page_fluid(   theme = bs_theme(version = 5),   DataGrid(     rows = head(dplyr::select(dplyr::starwars, name, height, mass))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {}  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Bootstrap CSS Conflicts
text: last resort, change Bootstrap version, wrap DataGrid container override MUI font-size classes explicit pixel values, scoped container : keeps fix scoped grid affect Bootstrap-styled elements app. Note spacing row height values MUI also use rem, minor sizing differences may still appear compared Bootstrap-free environment.
code: library(shiny) library(muiDataGrid)  ui <- fluidPage(   tags$head(     tags$style(HTML("       #datagrid-container .MuiDataGrid-root,       #datagrid-container .MuiDataGrid-columnHeaderTitle,       #datagrid-container .MuiDataGrid-cell,       #datagrid-container .MuiTablePagination-root,       #datagrid-container .MuiTablePagination-displayedRows,       #datagrid-container .MuiTablePagination-selectLabel,       #datagrid-container .MuiSelect-select {         font-size: 14px !important;       }     "))   ),   tags$div(     id = "datagrid-container",     DataGrid(       rows = head(dplyr::select(dplyr::starwars, name, height, mass))     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {}  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Column Definition
text: Column definitions Data Grid specify data displayed, formatted, interacted . column unique field name various properties control behavior. Free tier . muiDataGrid wraps open-source MUI X Data Grid (Community) component. Community-tier features available. Properties belong commercial Pro Premium tiers — column pinning (pinnable), row grouping, aggregation, tree-data master-detail layouts, Excel export — bundled effect pass . See MUI X feature comparison features belong plan.

title: Column Definition
text: unique identifier column (required). Must match field row data. DataGrid() internally handles mandatory "id" argument required MUI Data Grid. dataset contain "id" column, one automatically added based row number using nrow(). "id" column already exists dataset, used -. Warning: dataframe already contains "id" column, value must unique — duplicate IDs cause unexpected behavior grid.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name"),     list(field = "height"),     list(field = "mass")   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: text displayed column header. Defaults field name specified.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", headerName = "Character Name"),     list(field = "height", headerName = "Height (cm)"),     list(field = "mass", headerName = "Weight (kg)")   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Tooltip text shown hovering column header.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       headerName = "Name",       description = "The character's full name"     ),     list(       field = "height",       headerName = "Height",       description = "Height in centimeters"     ),     list(       field = "mass",       headerName = "Mass",       description = "Weight in kilograms"     )   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: column type affects formatting default behavior. Available types: - "string" (default) - "number" - "date" - "dateTime" - "boolean" - "singleSelect"
code: starwars_typed <- starwars |>   select(name, height, birth_year) |>   mutate(is_tall = height > 180) |>   head()  DataGrid(   rows = starwars_typed,   columns = list(     list(field = "name", type = "string", headerName = "Name", width = 200),     list(field = "height", type = "number", headerName = "Height", width = 120),     list(field = "birth_year", type = "number", headerName = "Birth Year", width = 120),     list(field = "is_tall", type = "boolean", headerName = "Tall?", width = 100)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Dates need special handling. Data Grid auto-detect date type data, — just importantly — R Date POSIXct values transmitted browser readable date strings. data frame serialized, Date cell loses class sent bare number (days since 1970-01-01), POSIXct sent seconds since epoch. date column fed number renders 1/1/1970, browser interprets milliseconds since epoch. reliable pattern therefore convert dates ISO 8601 character strings R first, declare column type = "date" ("dateTime") add valueGetter turns string back JavaScript Date: valueGetter makes MUI’s date-picker filter operators (, , onOrAfter, …) locale-aware valueFormatter formatting work. timestamps, format ISO date-time string use type = "dateTime": need dates displayed sorted (date-picker filter), simplest option keep ISO character strings leave column default "string" type — ISO strings already sort chronologically.
code: events <- data.frame(   event = c("A New Hope", "The Empire Strikes Back", "Return of the Jedi"),   release = as.Date(c("1977-05-25", "1980-05-21", "1983-05-25")),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  events$release <- format(events$release)  # ISO strings: "1977-05-25"  DataGrid(   rows = events,   columns = list(     list(field = "event", headerName = "Film", width = 220),     list(       field = "release",       headerName = "Release Date",       type = "date",       width = 160,       valueGetter = JS("function(value) { return value ? new Date(value) : null; }")     )   ) ) logs <- data.frame(   message = c("Started", "Finished"),   ts = as.POSIXct(c("2024-03-15 09:30:00", "2024-03-15 17:45:00"), tz = "UTC"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  logs$ts <- format(logs$ts, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")  # "2024-03-15T09:30:00Z"  DataGrid(   rows = logs,   columns = list(     list(field = "message", headerName = "Event", width = 160),     list(       field = "ts",       headerName = "Timestamp",       type = "dateTime",       width = 200,       valueGetter = JS("function(value) { return value ? new Date(value) : null; }")     )   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Fixed column width pixels.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", width = 250),     list(field = "height", width = 120),     list(field = "mass", width = 120)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Flexible column width proportion available space. used width.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", flex = 2),     list(field = "height", flex = 1),     list(field = "mass", flex = 1)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Constraints column width using flex resizing.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", flex = 1, minWidth = 150),     list(field = "height", flex = 1, minWidth = 80, maxWidth = 120),     list(field = "mass", flex = 1, minWidth = 80, maxWidth = 120)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Text alignment cell content header.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",        headerAlign = "left",        align = "left",        width = 200     ),     list(       field = "height",        headerAlign = "center",        align = "right",        width = 120     ),     list(       field = "mass",        headerAlign = "center",        align = "right",        width = 120     )   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Hide column keeping data.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", width = 200),     list(field = "height", width = 120),     list(field = "mass", width = 120, hide = TRUE),     list(field = "species", width = 150)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Enable disable sorting column (default: TRUE).
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", sortable = TRUE, width = 200),     list(field = "height", sortable = TRUE, width = 120),     list(field = "mass", sortable = FALSE, width = 120)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Enable disable filtering column (default: TRUE).
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", filterable = TRUE, width = 200),     list(field = "height", filterable = TRUE, width = 120),     list(field = "mass", filterable = FALSE, width = 150)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Make column editable (default: FALSE).
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", editable = FALSE, width = 200),     list(field = "height", editable = TRUE, width = 120),     list(field = "mass", editable = TRUE, width = 120)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Enable column resizing dragging borders (default: TRUE).
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", resizable = TRUE, width = 200),     list(field = "height", resizable = TRUE, width = 120),     list(field = "mass", resizable = FALSE, width = 120)   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Format displayed value without changing underlying data.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", width = 200),     list(       field = "height",       headerName = "Height",       width = 150,       valueFormatter = JS("function(value) { return value ? value + ' cm' : 'Unknown'; }")     ),     list(       field = "mass",       headerName = "Mass",       width = 150,       valueFormatter = JS("function(value) { return value ? value + ' kg' : 'Unknown'; }")     )   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Compute derive values row data.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", width = 150),     list(field = "height", width = 100),     list(field = "mass", width = 100),     list(       field = "bmi",       headerName = "BMI",       width = 100,       type = "number",       valueGetter = JS("         function(value, row) {           if (row.height && row.mass) {             const heightInM = row.height / 100;             return Math.round(row.mass / (heightInM * heightInM) * 10) / 10;           }           return null;         }       ")     )   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Control edited values stored (editable columns):
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name"),     list(       field = "height",       editable = TRUE,       valueSetter = JS("         function(value, row) {           const numValue = Number(value);           if (!isNaN(numValue) && numValue > 0) {             row.height = numValue;             return row;           }           return row; // Return unchanged if invalid         }       ")     ),     list(field = "mass")   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Custom cell rendering React components:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       renderCell = JS("         function(params) {           return React.createElement('strong', {}, params.value);         }       ")     ),     list(field = "height"),     list(field = "mass")   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Custom header rendering:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       renderHeader = JS("         function(params) {           return React.createElement('strong', {}, params.colDef.headerName || params.field);         }       ")     ),     list(field = "height"),     list(field = "mass")   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: Group multiple columns common header:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |>      select(name, height, mass, hair_color, skin_color) |>      head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name"),     list(field = "height"),     list(field = "mass"),     list(field = "hair_color"),     list(field = "skin_color")   ),   columnGroupingModel = list(     list(       groupId = "physical",       headerName = "Physical Attributes",       children = list(         list(field = "height"),         list(field = "mass")       )     ),     list(       groupId = "appearance",       headerName = "Appearance",       children = list(         list(field = "hair_color"),         list(field = "skin_color")       )     )   ) )

title: Column Definition
text: ’s comprehensive example using many column properties:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, hair_color, skin_color) |> head(10),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       headerName = "Character Name",       description = "Name of the Star Wars character",       width = 180,       sortable = TRUE,       filterable = TRUE,       hideable = FALSE,       align = "left"     ),     list(       field = "height",       headerName = "Height",       description = "Height in centimeters",       type = "number",       width = 100,       align = "center",       headerAlign = "center",       valueFormatter = JS("function(value) { return value ? value + ' cm' : 'N/A'; }")     ),     list(       field = "mass",       headerName = "Mass",       description = "Mass in kilograms",       type = "number",       width = 100,       align = "center",       headerAlign = "center",       valueFormatter = JS("function(value) { return value ? value + ' kg' : 'N/A'; }")     )   ),   initialState = list(     pagination = list(       paginationModel = list(pageSize = 5)     )   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: Column dimensions control columns sized within data grid. grid provides flexible options fixed dynamic column widths.

title: Column Dimensions
text: simplest way control column width set fixed pixel value:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", width = 250),     list(field = "height", width = 100),     list(field = "mass", width = 100)   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: width specified, columns default 100 pixels wide.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head()   # All columns will be 100px wide )

title: Column Dimensions
text: Use flex property make columns grow proportionally fill available space. flex value determines proportion available space column takes.

title: Column Dimensions
text: columns share available space equally.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", flex = 1),     list(field = "height", flex = 1),     list(field = "mass", flex = 1)   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: Columns higher flex values take proportionally space:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, hair_color) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", flex = 3),        # Takes 3/5 of flexible space     list(field = "height", flex = 1),      # Takes 1/5 of flexible space     list(field = "mass", flex = 1),        # Takes 1/5 of flexible space     list(field = "hair_color", width = 150) # Fixed width, not flexible   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: can mix fixed width columns flex columns: fixed columns maintain width, flex columns share remaining space.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, hair_color) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", flex = 2),     list(field = "height", width = 100),    # Fixed     list(field = "mass", width = 100),      # Fixed     list(field = "hair_color", flex = 1)   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: Set minimum width constraint prevent columns becoming narrow: grid resized smaller, columns shrink minWidth.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", flex = 1, minWidth = 150),     list(field = "height", flex = 1, minWidth = 80),     list(field = "mass", flex = 1, minWidth = 80)   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: Set maximum width constraint prevent columns becoming wide:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", flex = 1, maxWidth = 200),     list(field = "height", flex = 1),     list(field = "mass", flex = 1)   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: Combine minimum maximum width precise control: ensures columns stay within reasonable range still flexible.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", flex = 1, minWidth = 120, maxWidth = 300),     list(field = "height", width = 100),     list(field = "mass", width = 100)   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: default, users can manually resize columns dragging column header edges:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head()   # Column resizing enabled by default )

title: Column Dimensions
text: Control columns can resized:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", resizable = TRUE),     list(field = "height", resizable = FALSE), # Cannot resize     list(field = "mass", resizable = FALSE)    # Cannot resize   ) )

title: Column Dimensions
text: Disable column resizing entire grid:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   disableColumnResize = TRUE )

title: Column Dimensions
text: can programmatically auto-size columns fit content. typically done grid’s API, can set initial widths based content length:
code: # Estimate widths based on content DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, hair_color) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", width = 180),        # Longer names     list(field = "height", width = 90),       # Short numbers     list(field = "hair_color", width = 120)   # Medium text   ) )

title: Custom with React JS
text: Using valueGetter valueFormatter: Using valueGetter renderCell:
code: # https://stackoverflow.com/a/79508826 library(dplyr)  # "id" is auto-generated if not present df <- starwars |>   select(1:2) |>   mutate(     id = row_number() )  DataGrid(   sx = list(height = 300),   rows = df,   columns = list(     list(       field = "name"     ),     list(       field = "height",       type = "number",       headerName = "Height",       description = "Height in meters",       valueGetter = JS("         function(value) {           if (value == null) { return ''; }           return value / 100 }"       ),       valueFormatter = JS("         function(value) {           if (value == '') { return ''; }           return `${value} cm`}"       )     )   ) ) library(shiny.react)  # "id" is auto-generated if not present df <- starwars |>   select(1:2) |>   mutate(     id = row_number() )  DataGrid(   sx = list(height = 300),   rows = df,   columns = list(     list(       field = "name"     ),     list(       field = "height",       valueGetter = JS("function(value) { return value / 100 }"),       renderCell = JS("function(params) { return `${params.value} m` }")     )   ) )

what: Custom table using React JS Components
title: Custom with React JS
text: don’t know React, suggest ask LLM create React function . examples created using help LLM: simple example add custom image table (.e. wrapping value styled “img” HTML tag): can create React function color values cell background following logic: can also use Google Material Icons loading font stylesheet htmltools::tags$link(): can add React function create custom progress bar: another example using React Rating component: , just ask LLM create React function according needs.
code: library(shiny.react)  df <- data.frame(   country = c("Switzerland", "France"),   img = c("https://flagsapi.com/CH/flat/64.png", "https://flagsapi.com/FR/flat/64.png") )  DataGrid(   sx = list(height = 300),   rows = df,   columns = list(     list(       field = "img",       headerName = "",       width = 10,       sortable = FALSE,       disableColumnMenu = TRUE,       renderCell = JS("         function(params) {            return React.createElement(             'img',             {               src: params.value,               alt: 'flag',               style: {                 width: '100%',                 height: '100%',                 objectFit: 'contain',                 display: 'block'               }             }           );         }"       )     ),     list(       field = "country"     )   ) ) df <- data.frame(   id = c(1,2,3,4,5),   value = c(1,2,3,4,5) )  DataGrid(   rows = df,   columns = list(     list(       field = "value",       renderCell = JS("         function(params) {          // Colored Value         const ColoredValue = ({ value }) => {           let bgColor = '#e8f5e9'; // light green           let textColor = '#2e7d32';                      if (value < 2) {             bgColor = '#ffebee'; // light red             textColor = '#c62828';           } else if (value < 3) {             bgColor = '#ffe0b2'; // light orange             textColor = '#e65100';           } else if (value < 4) {             bgColor = '#fff9c4'; // light yellow             textColor = '#f57f17';           }           return React.createElement(             'div',             {               style: {                 width: '100%',                 height: '100%',                 display: 'flex',                 alignItems: 'center',                 justifyContent: 'center',                 backgroundColor: bgColor,                 color: textColor,                 fontWeight: '600',                 fontSize: '13px',                 margin: '-8px -16px', // Negative margin to counteract cell padding                 padding: '8px 16px'   // Add padding back to maintain text position               }             },             value.toFixed(1)           );         };         return React.createElement(ColoredValue, { value: params.value })         }")     )   ) ) df <- data.frame(   id = c(1,2,3,4,5),   value = c(1,2,3,4,5) )  htmltools::tagList(   htmltools::tags$link(     rel = "stylesheet",     href = "https://fonts.googleapis.com/icon?family=Material+Icons+Outlined"   ),   DataGrid(     rows = df,     columns = list(       list(         field = "value",         renderCell = JS("           function(params) {           const ColoredValue = ({ value }) => {             let bgColor = '#e8f5e9'; // light green             let textColor = '#2e7d32';             let icon = 'sentiment_very_satisfied'; // very happy              if (value < 2) {               bgColor = '#ffebee'; // light red               textColor = '#c62828';               icon = 'sentiment_very_dissatisfied'; // very sad             } else if (value < 3) {               bgColor = '#ffe0b2'; // light orange               textColor = '#e65100';               icon = 'sentiment_dissatisfied'; // dissatisfied             } else if (value < 4) {               bgColor = '#fff9c4'; // light yellow               textColor = '#f57f17';               icon = 'sentiment_satisfied'; // satisfied             }              return React.createElement(               'div',               {                 style: {                   width: '100%',                   height: '100%',                   display: 'flex',                   alignItems: 'center',                   justifyContent: 'center',                   gap: '8px',                   backgroundColor: bgColor,                   color: textColor,                   fontWeight: '600',                   fontSize: '13px',                   margin: '-8px -16px',                   padding: '8px 16px'                 }               },               React.createElement('span', {                 className: 'material-icons-outlined',                 style: { fontSize: '18px' }               }, icon),               value.toFixed(1)             );           };           return React.createElement(ColoredValue, { value: params.value })           }")       )     )   ) ) |>   htmltools::browsable() # show in viewer df <- data.frame(   id = c(1, 2, 3),   value = c(20, 60, 80) )  DataGrid(   rows = df,   columns = list(     list(       field = "value",       renderCell = JS("         function(params) {          // Progress Bar Component         const ProgressBar = ({ value }) => {           return React.createElement(             'div',             {               style: {                 width: '100%',                 height: '100%',                 display: 'flex',                 alignItems: 'center'               }             },             React.createElement(               'div',               {                 style: {                   width: '100%',                   height: '20px',                   backgroundColor: '#e0e0e0',                   overflow: 'hidden',                   position: 'relative'                 }               },               React.createElement('div', {                 style: {                   width: `${value}%`,                   height: '100%',                   backgroundColor: value < 30 ? '#f44336' : value < 70 ? '#ff9800' : '#4caf50',                   transition: 'width 0.3s ease'                 }               }),               React.createElement(                 'span',                 {                   style: {                     position: 'absolute',                     top: '50%',                     left: '50%',                     transform: 'translate(-50%, -50%)',                     fontSize: '11px',                     fontWeight: '600',                     color: '#333',                     textShadow: '0 0 2px rgba(255,255,255,0.8)'                   }                 },                 `${value}%`               )             )           );         };         return React.createElement(ProgressBar, { value: params.value })         }")     )   ) ) df <- data.frame(   id = c(1, 2, 3),   value = c(1, 3, 6) )  DataGrid(   rows = df,   columns = list(     list(       field = "value",       renderCell = JS("         function(params) {          // Rating Component         const Rating = ({ value }) => {           const maxStars = 5;           const filledStars = Math.max(1, Math.min(5, Math.round(value))); // Clamp value between 1-5                      return React.createElement(             'div',             {               style: {                 width: '100%',                 height: '100%',                 display: 'flex',                 alignItems: 'center',                 justifyContent: 'center',                 gap: '4px'               }             },             ...Array.from({ length: maxStars }, (_, index) => {               const isFilled = index < filledStars;               return React.createElement(                 'span',                 {                   key: index,                   style: {                     fontSize: '18px',                     color: isFilled ? '#ffc107' : '#e0e0e0'                   }                 },                 '★'               );             })           );         };         return React.createElement(Rating, { value: params.value })         }")     )   ) )

title: Layout
text: Data Grid offers multiple options control layout.

title: Layout
text: can specify fixed height using CSS sx prop:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(10),   sx = list(height = 300) )

title: Layout
text: Data Grid can automatically adjust height fit content using autoHeight prop:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(10),   autoHeight = TRUE )

title: Layout
text: default, Data Grid takes full width container. columns flex fill available space.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(10) )

title: Layout
text: can specify fixed width using CSS sx prop:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(10),   sx = list(width = 600) )

title: Layout
text: can add pagination adjust page size using initialState (5 rows per page):
code: library(dplyr)  DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(10),   pageSizeOptions = c(5, 10, 25),   initialState = list(     pagination = list(       paginationModel = list(pageSize = 5)     )   ) )

title: Layout
text: Set showToolbar = TRUE display toolbar, includes column, filter, density, export controls. Export button can download current data CSV file. See Localization vignette fix accented characters (ä becomes Ã¤, è becomes Ã¨) CSV exports Excel.
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(10),   showToolbar = TRUE )

title: Localization
text: Localizing grid involves two independent concerns: UI text — labels component (“Rows per page”, column menu items, filter operators, …). Controlled localeText prop. Value formatting — data cells formatted (thousands separators, decimal marks, dates). Controlled per column valueFormatter. separate axes: can show German UI text formatting numbers Swiss way, vice versa. article covers .

what: Translating the UI text with localeText
title: Localization
text: MUI X Data Grid ships ready-made translations many languages. muiDataGrid bundles , can point localeText locale object using path documented React component: translates whole grid — pagination (“Zeilen pro Seite:”), column menus, filter panel, export menu selection footer — one shot. reference 1:1 mirror React idiom localeText={deDE.components.MuiDataGrid.defaultProps.localeText}. use another language, swap deDE bundled locale code, example frFR itIT:
code: DataGrid(   rows = head(iris),   localeText = JS(     "window.jsmodule['@mui/x-data-grid/locales'].deDE.components.MuiDataGrid.defaultProps.localeText"   ) ) # French UI DataGrid(   rows = head(iris),   localeText = JS(     "window.jsmodule['@mui/x-data-grid/locales'].frFR.components.MuiDataGrid.defaultProps.localeText"   ) )

previous_headings: Localization > Translating the UI text with localeText
title: Localization
text: MUI X provides following codes. regional variants (e.g. deCH Swiss German) — localeText controls wording , wording identical across regions. Regional differences matter value formatting (see ). Switzerland, use base-language code: deDE (Swiss German), frFR (Romandy) itIT (Ticino).
code: arSD beBY bgBG bnBD caES csCZ daDK deDE elGR enUS esES faIR fiFI frFR heIL hrHR huHU hyAM idID isIS itIT jaJP koKR nbNO nlNL nnNO plPL ptBR ptPT roRO ruRU skSK svSE thTH trTR ukUA urPK viVN zhCN zhHK zhTW

previous_headings: Localization > Translating the UI text with localeText
title: Localization
text: want override handful strings, pass localeText named list instead. “Rows per page” lives nested MuiTablePagination key:
code: DataGrid(   rows = head(iris),   localeText = list(     MuiTablePagination = list(labelRowsPerPage = "Zeilen pro Seite:"),     columnMenuSortAsc  = "Aufsteigend sortieren",     columnMenuSortDesc = "Absteigend sortieren",     columnMenuFilter   = "Filtern",     toolbarExport      = "Exportieren"   ) )

title: Localization
text: column type = "number", value formatted locale-aware way. German means thousands separator — rendered narrow space — 2005 appears 2 005. identifiers years usually want suppress . Use valueFormatter Intl.NumberFormat useGrouping: false: CSV/Excel export uses formatted value, valueFormatter fixes -screen cell downloaded file one place — need separate export configuration. Alternatively, number purely label never sort filter numerically, declaring type = "string" avoids numeric formatting.
code: DataGrid(   rows = data.frame(country = c("CH", "DE", "FR"), year = c(2005L, 1990L, 2021L)),   columns = list(     list(field = "country", headerName = "Country", width = 120),     list(       field = "year",       headerName = "Year",       type = "number",       width = 120,       valueFormatter = JS(         "function(v){ return v == null ? '' : v.toLocaleString('de-DE', { useGrouping: false }); }"       )     )   ) )

title: Localization
text: Unlike localeText, Intl locale passed valueFormatter support regional variants. Use de-CH Swiss separator (1'000):
code: DataGrid(   rows = data.frame(city = c("Zürich", "Genève"), population = c(421878L, 203856L)),   columns = list(     list(field = "city", headerName = "City", width = 140),     list(       field = "population",       headerName = "Population",       type = "number",       width = 160,       valueFormatter = JS(         "function(v){ return v == null ? '' : v.toLocaleString('de-CH'); }"       )     )   ) )

title: Localization
text: German UI text (deDE) Swiss number formatting (de-CH):
code: DataGrid(   rows = data.frame(city = c("Zürich", "Genève"), population = c(421878L, 203856L)),   columns = list(     list(field = "city", headerName = "Stadt", width = 140),     list(       field = "population",       headerName = "Einwohner",       type = "number",       width = 160,       valueFormatter = JS(         "function(v){ return v == null ? '' : v.toLocaleString('de-CH'); }"       )     )   ),   localeText = JS(     "window.jsmodule['@mui/x-data-grid/locales'].deDE.components.MuiDataGrid.defaultProps.localeText"   ) )

what: Fixing accented characters in CSV exports
title: Localization
text: default, MUI X writes CSV UTF-8 without byte-order mark (BOM). spreadsheet applications — notably Excel Windows — assume legacy encoding (Windows-1252) mangle accented characters: ä becomes Ã¤, è becomes Ã¨, . exported file actually correct UTF-8; application’s encoding guess wrong. fix , prepend UTF-8 BOM setting utf8WithBom = TRUE toolbar’s csvOptions, signals encoding spreadsheet application: csvOptions list accepts delimiter — German French versions Excel expect semicolon rather comma, delimiter = ";" can improve compatibility. options apply DataGrid() DataGridServer(), since extra props passed straight underlying grid.
code: DataGrid(   rows = data.frame(     city = c("Altstätten", "Genève", "Zürich"),     canton = c("St. Gallen", "Genève", "Zürich")   ),   showToolbar = TRUE,   slotProps = list(     toolbar = list(       csvOptions = list(utf8WithBom = TRUE)     )   ) )

title: Row Definition
text: Rows fundamental data units Data Grid. guide covers feed data grid work row identifiers.

title: Row Definition
text: grid automatically handles R data frames tibbles rows prop:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head() )

title: Row Definition
text: muiDataGrid package automatically generates unique IDs rows, don’t need create id column manually:
code: # No need to add id column - it's handled automatically DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head() )

title: Row Definition
text: large datasets, consider using pagination:
code: # Show a subset of data DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> head(10),   initialState = list(     pagination = list(       paginationModel = list(pageSize = 5)     )   ) )

title: Row Height
text: Data Grid provides flexible options controlling row height, static heights dynamic sizing based content.

title: Row Height
text: Set uniform height rows:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   rowHeight = 80 )

title: Row Height
text: default row height 52 pixels:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head()   # Default rowHeight = 52 )

title: Row Height
text: Customize height column header using columnHeaderHeight prop:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   columnHeaderHeight = 80 )

title: Row Height
text: Set different heights different rows using function:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   getRowHeight = JS("     function(params) {       // Make every third row taller       return params.id % 3 === 0 ? 80 : 52;     }   ") )

title: Row Height
text: Use ‘auto’ let rows expand fit content:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, films) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", width = 180),     list(field = "films", width = 400)   ),   getRowHeight = JS("function() { return 'auto'; }") )

title: Row Height
text: Provide estimated height better scrolling performance:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, films) |> head(10),   columns = list(     list(field = "name", width = 180),     list(field = "films", width = 400)   ),   getRowHeight = JS("function() { return 'auto'; }"),   getEstimatedRowHeight = JS("function() { return 80; }") )

title: Row Height
text: Use predefined density settings quickly adjust row height: Density values affect row height: - “compact”: ~36px - “standard”: ~52px (default) - “comfortable”: ~65px
code: # Compact density DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   density = "compact" ) # Standard density (default) DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   density = "standard" ) # Comfortable density DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   density = "comfortable" )

title: Row Height
text: Add vertical spacing rows:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   getRowSpacing = JS("     function(params) {       return {         top: params.isFirstVisible ? 0 : 5,         bottom: params.isLastVisible ? 0 : 5       };     }   ") )

title: Row Height
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(10),   sx = list(     "& .MuiDataGrid-row" = list(       borderBottom = "2px solid #e0e0e0",       marginBottom = "4px"     )   ) )

title: Row Selection
text: grid supports checkbox click-based row selection. Selected row IDs sent Shiny via onRowSelectionModelChange, making easy react user selections server. examples page require running Shiny session.

title: Row Selection
text: Enable checkboxes checkboxSelection = TRUE. Use onRowSelectionModelChange send selected IDs Shiny input whenever selection changes: input$selected_rows numeric vector selected row IDs (integers assigned automatically muiDataGrid). updates real time user checks unchecks rows.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(muiDataGrid) library(dplyr)  df <- starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(10)  ui <- page_fluid(   reactOutput("grid"),   verbatimTextOutput("selection_info") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$grid <- renderReact({     DataGrid(       rows = df,       checkboxSelection = TRUE,       onRowSelectionModelChange = JS(         "(model) => Shiny.setInputValue('selected_rows', Array.from(model.ids))"       )     )   })    output$selection_info <- renderPrint({     ids <- input$selected_rows     if (is.null(ids) || length(ids) == 0) {       cat("No rows selected.")     } else {       cat("Selected row IDs:", paste(ids, collapse = ", "), "\n")       cat("Count:", length(ids))     }   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Row Selection
text: common pattern use selected IDs filter source data frame display process selected records: Note: muiDataGrid assigns row IDs starting 1 using seq_len(nrow(rows)). Use .integer(input$selected_rows) index back original data frame.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(muiDataGrid) library(dplyr)  df <- starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species, homeworld) |> head(20)  ui <- page_fluid(   h4("Select characters to compare"),   reactOutput("grid"),   hr(),   h4("Selected characters"),   tableOutput("selected_table") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$grid <- renderReact({     DataGrid(       rows = df,       checkboxSelection = TRUE,       onRowSelectionModelChange = JS(         "(model) => Shiny.setInputValue('selected_rows', Array.from(model.ids))"       )     )   })    output$selected_table <- renderTable({     req(input$selected_rows)     df[as.integer(input$selected_rows), ]   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Row Selection
text: Disable multi-row selection disableMultipleRowSelection = TRUE. useful one record selected time, example display details sidebar:
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(muiDataGrid) library(dplyr)  df <- starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species, homeworld, birth_year) |> head(20)  ui <- page_fluid(   layout_columns(     col_widths = c(8, 4),     reactOutput("grid"),     card(       card_header("Character details"),       uiOutput("details")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$grid <- renderReact({     DataGrid(       rows = df,       checkboxSelection = TRUE,       disableMultipleRowSelection = TRUE,       onRowSelectionModelChange = JS(         "(model) => Shiny.setInputValue('selected_rows', Array.from(model.ids))"       )     )   })    output$details <- renderUI({     req(input$selected_rows)     row <- df[as.integer(input$selected_rows[1]), ]     tagList(       tags$b(row$name), tags$br(),       tags$span("Height: ", row$height, " cm"), tags$br(),       tags$span("Mass: ", row$mass, " kg"), tags$br(),       tags$span("Homeworld: ", row$homeworld)     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Row Selection
text: Initialise grid rows already selected passing rowSelectionModel:
code: DataGrid(   rows = df,   checkboxSelection = TRUE,   rowSelectionModel = JS('{ type: "include", ids: new Set([1, 3]) }'),   onRowSelectionModelChange = JS(     "(model) => Shiny.setInputValue('selected_rows', Array.from(model.ids))"   ) )

title: Row Selection
text: Row selection works way DataGridServer(). current page sent browser, pass keepNonExistentRowsSelected = TRUE rows selected previous page lost user navigates: Tip: server-side pagination, input$selected_rows always reflects full selection across pages, just visible page.
code: library(shiny) library(bslib) library(muiDataGrid)  all_data <- data.frame(   id    = 1:500,   label = paste("Row", 1:500),   value = round(runif(500, 0, 100), 1) )  ui <- page_fluid(   reactOutput("grid"),   verbatimTextOutput("selection_info") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$grid <- renderReact({     DataGridServer(       inputId = "grid_params",       rows = all_data,       initialPageSize = 5L,       pageSizeOptions = c(5L, 10L, 20L),       checkboxSelection = TRUE,       keepNonExistentRowsSelected = TRUE,       onRowSelectionModelChange = JS(         "(model) => Shiny.setInputValue('selected_rows', Array.from(model.ids))"       )     )   })    output$selection_info <- renderPrint({     ids <- input$selected_rows     if (is.null(ids) || length(ids) == 0) {       cat("No rows selected.")     } else {       cat("Selected IDs:", paste(sort(ids), collapse = ", "), "\n")       cat("Count:", length(ids))     }   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Row Spanning
text: Row spanning allows cells merge vertically across multiple rows contain identical consecutive values. feature useful creating hierarchical grouped displays.

title: Row Spanning
text: Enable row spanning rowSpanning prop
code: # Group characters by species sw_grouped <- starwars |>   select(name, species, homeworld, height) |>   filter(!is.na(species), species %in% c("Human", "Droid")) |>   arrange(species, homeworld) |>   head(12) |>   mutate(id = row_number())  DataGrid(   rows = sw_grouped,   rowSpanning = TRUE )

title: Row Spanning
code: # Add group summary sw_with_summary <- sw_grouped |>   group_by(species) |>   mutate(     species_count = n(),     species_label = paste0(species, " (", species_count, ")")   ) |>   ungroup()  columns_summary <- list(   list(     field = "species_label",     headerName = "Species",     width = 150   ),   list(field = "name", headerName = "Name", width = 150),   list(field = "homeworld", headerName = "Homeworld", width = 120),   list(field = "height", headerName = "Height", width = 100) )  DataGrid(   rows = sw_with_summary,   columns = columns_summary,   rowSpanning = TRUE )

title: Server-Side Data
text: Experimental — specific package. DataGridServer() processGridParams() equivalent MUI X Data Grid. MUI ships building blocks (paginationMode = "server" plus callbacks) leaves server-side data layer developer; package supplies layer R. result two functions experimental encode number opinionated decisions (described ) may change future releases. depend current behaviour, pin package version. client-side DataGrid() thin wrapper real MUI component experimental.

title: Server-Side Data
text: default, DataGrid() sends rows browser . Pagination, sorting, filtering handled entirely JavaScript client side. works well small datasets, becomes problem large ones: Performance: sending thousands rows JSON slows initial page load. Memory: browser must hold entire dataset memory. Security: data accessible browser, even rows currently displayed. server-side data, rows current page sent browser. Pagination, sorting, filtering handled R server. keeps app fast regardless dataset size.

title: Server-Side Data
text: DataGridServer() Shiny-component. uses custom React component : Manages pagination, sort, filter state internally browser. Sends current state R via input$<inputId> whenever user changes page, sorts column, applies filter. R processes data sends back matching rows current page.

title: Server-Side Data
text: DataGridServer() supports two modes: Automatic mode (default): pass full dataset via rows. Pagination, sorting, filtering handled internally processGridParams(). simplest option works well data fits R memory. Manual mode: pass pre-sliced rows together explicit rowCount. Use need full control — example, query database apply custom filtering logic.

title: Server-Side Data
text: minimal server-side DataGrid requires reactOutput() UI DataGridServer() server. Pass full dataset via rows — exactly like DataGrid() — pagination, sorting, filtering handled automatically: Note: initialPageSize must included pageSizeOptions — error raised . also sets page size first render, grid sent state R.
code: library(shiny) # 100'000 rows all_data <- data.frame(   id = 1:100000,   label = paste("Row", 1:100000),   value = 1:100000,   group = rep(c("A", "B", "C", "D"), each = 25000) )  # Very slow with big data # DataGrid( #   rows = all_data, #   initialState = list( #     pagination = list( #       paginationModel = list(pageSize = 5) #     ) #   ) # )  ui <- div(   reactOutput("grid") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$grid <- renderReact({     DataGridServer(       inputId = "grid_params",       rows = all_data,       initialPageSize = 5L,       pageSizeOptions = c(5L, 10L, 25L)     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Server-Side Data
text: Renders DataGrid server-side pagination, sorting, filtering.

title: Server-Side Data
text: Applies pagination, sorting, filtering data frame based input$<inputId>. Use directly need custom server-side logic (e.g. database queries) DataGridServer() handle automatically. Returns list : rows: data frame current page rowCount: total number matching rows (filtering, pagination)

title: Server-Side Data
text: processGridParams() handles following MUI filter operators: Unrecognized operators pass rows (filtering) emit warning. need operators listed , use manual mode handle filtering . Note: String operators (contains, equals, startsWith, endsWith, ) case-insensitive. operator case-sensitive, matching MUI’s default behavior. filtering reimplemented R, edge cases (locale, accents) can differ MUI’s client-side filter.

what: Opinionated behaviour to be aware of
title: Server-Side Data
text: server-side layer bespoke (see experimental note top), makes decisions . Knowing avoids surprises: Mode chosen rowCount. Supplying rowCount switches manual mode rows treated already-sliced page — passing full dataset rowCount set show everything page one pagination. Omit rowCount automatic mode. (guard, DataGridServer() warns nrow(rows) exceeds rowCount.) Sorting filtering resets first page. Missing values always sort last, regardless sort direction. Row ids must stable selection. rows id column, ids generated positionally (1..n current filtered/sorted result), record gets different id sort filter change. use checkboxSelection server-side data, include stable, unique id column rows selections survive sorting filtering.

title: Server-Side Data
text: can define custom columns way DataGrid():
code: library(dplyr)  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$grid <- renderReact({     result <- processGridParams(dplyr::starwars, input$grid_params, pageSize = 5L)      DataGridServer(       inputId = "grid_params",       rows = result$rows,       rowCount = result$rowCount,       initialPageSize = 5L,       columns = list(         list(field = "name", headerName = "Name", flex = 1),         list(field = "height", headerName = "Height (cm)", type = "number", width = 120),         list(field = "mass", headerName = "Mass (kg)", type = "number", width = 120),         list(field = "gender", headerName = "Gender", width = 120)       )     )   }) }

title: Server-Side Data
text: manual mode, grid can show loading overlay data fetched. Use reactiveVal track loading state: useful data source slow (e.g. database query API call).
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   loading <- reactiveVal(FALSE)    grid_data <- reactive({     loading(TRUE)     on.exit(loading(FALSE))     result <- processGridParams(all_data, input$grid_params, pageSize = 50L)     result   })    output$grid <- renderReact({     result <- grid_data()     DataGridServer(       inputId = "grid_params",       rows = result$rows,       rowCount = result$rowCount,       loading = loading(),       initialPageSize = 50L,       pageSizeOptions = c(50L, 100L)     )   }) }

title: Server-Side Data
text: grid state available input$<inputId> list three elements: pagination_model: list page (0-indexed) pageSize sort_model: list sort items, field sort (“asc” “desc”) filter_model: list items, containing field, operator, value; logicOperator (“” “”) can use display diagnostics trigger reactive logic:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$grid <- renderReact({     result <- processGridParams(all_data, input$grid_params)     DataGridServer("grid_params", rows = result$rows, rowCount = result$rowCount)   })    observe({     params <- input$grid_params     if (!is.null(params)) {       cat("Page:", params$pagination_model$page, "\n")       cat("Page size:", params$pagination_model$pageSize, "\n")     }   }) }

title: Server-Side Data
text: processGridParams() covers common sorting filtering operators. need custom logic (e.g. querying database), can skip handle input$<inputId> directly:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$grid <- renderReact({     params <- input$grid_params      page      <- if (!is.null(params)) params$pagination_model$page     else 0     page_size <- if (!is.null(params)) params$pagination_model$pageSize  else 25      # Custom database query     result <- DBI::dbGetQuery(con, sprintf(       "SELECT * FROM my_table ORDER BY id LIMIT %d OFFSET %d",       page_size, page * page_size     ))     total <- DBI::dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM my_table")[[1]]      DataGridServer(       inputId = "grid_params",       rows = result,       rowCount = total,       initialPageSize = 25L,       pageSizeOptions = c(25L, 50L, 100L)     )   }) }

title: Styling Basics
text: Data Grid CSS can easily customized using various approaches. guide covers fundamental styling methods available.

title: Styling Basics
text: one-styles, sx prop can used. lets apply simple complex customizations directly Data Grid element. keys accepted can CSS property well custom properties provided MUI.

previous_headings: Styling Basics > Using the sx Prop
title: Styling Basics
text: Apply simple styles like margin padding:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   sx = list(     m = 2  # margin: 2 * 8px = 16px   ) )

previous_headings: Styling Basics > Using the sx Prop
what: Advanced Styling with Borders and Shadows
title: Styling Basics
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   sx = list(     boxShadow = 2,     border = 2,     borderColor = "primary.light",     "& .MuiDataGrid-cell:hover" = list(       color = "primary.main"     )   ) )

previous_headings: Styling Basics > Using the sx Prop
title: Styling Basics
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   sx = list(     backgroundColor = "#f5f5f5",     "& .MuiDataGrid-columnHeaders" = list(       color = "blue",       fontSize = 20     ),     "& .MuiDataGrid-cell" = list(       borderBottom = "1px solid #e0e0e0"     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: column definition provides properties apply styles column headers:

title: Styling Basics
text: Apply custom class names column headers:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       headerName = "Character Name",       headerClassName = "custom-header",       width = 200     ),     list(       field = "height",       headerName = "Height (cm)",       headerClassName = "custom-header",       width = 120     ),     list(       field = "mass",       headerName = "Mass (kg)",       headerClassName = "custom-header",       width = 120     )   ),   sx = list(     "& .custom-header" = list(       backgroundColor = "#7b71cd",       fontWeight = "bold"     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: Align content column headers:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       headerName = "Character",       headerAlign = "left",       width = 200     ),     list(       field = "height",       headerName = "Height",       headerAlign = "center",       width = 120     ),     list(       field = "mass",       headerName = "Mass",       headerAlign = "right",       width = 120     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: getRowClassName prop applies custom CSS classes rows based data:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   getRowClassName = JS("     function(params) {       if (params.row.species === 'Droid') {         return 'droid-row';       } else if (params.row.species === 'Human') {         return 'human-row';       }       return '';     }   "),   sx = list(     "& .droid-row" = list(       backgroundColor = "#e3f2fd",       "&:hover" = list(         backgroundColor = "#bbdefb"       )     ),     "& .human-row" = list(       backgroundColor = "#fff3e0",       "&:hover" = list(         backgroundColor = "#ffe0b2"       )     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: Style rows based numeric values:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> head(),   getRowClassName = JS("     function(params) {       if (params.row.height > 190) {         return 'tall-character';       } else if (params.row.height < 100) {         return 'short-character';       }       return '';     }   "),   sx = list(     "& .tall-character" = list(       backgroundColor = "#c8e6c9",       fontWeight = "bold"     ),     "& .short-character" = list(       backgroundColor = "#ffccbc"     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: Apply CSS classes cells specific column:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |>      select(name, height, mass) |>     mutate(bmi = mass / (height/100)^2) |>     head(15),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       headerName = "Name",       width = 180,       cellClassName = "name-cell"     ),     list(       field = "height",       headerName = "Height",       width = 100     ),     list(       field = "mass",       headerName = "Mass",       width = 100     ),     list(       field = "bmi",       headerName = "BMI",       width = 100,       cellClassName = JS("         function(params) {           if (params.value == null) return '';           if (params.value > 30) return 'high-bmi';           if (params.value < 18.5) return 'low-bmi';           return 'normal-bmi';         }       ")     )   ),   sx = list(     "& .name-cell" = list(       fontWeight = "500"     ),     "& .high-bmi" = list(       backgroundColor = "#ffcdd2",       color = "#c62828"     ),     "& .low-bmi" = list(       backgroundColor = "#fff9c4",       color = "#f57f17"     ),     "& .normal-bmi" = list(       backgroundColor = "#c8e6c9",       color = "#2e7d32"     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: Apply conditional styling cells across columns:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass) |> filter(is.na(mass)) |> head(),   getCellClassName = JS("     function(params) {       if (params.field === 'name') return '';       if (params.value == null) return 'missing-value';       return '';     }   "),   sx = list(     "& .missing-value" = list(       backgroundColor = "lightblue",       fontStyle = "italic"     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: Use align property change alignment cell content:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       headerName = "Character",       align = "left",       headerAlign = "left",       width = 180     ),     list(       field = "height",       headerName = "Height",       align = "center",       headerAlign = "center",       width = 120     ),     list(       field = "mass",       headerName = "Mass",       align = "right",       headerAlign = "right",       width = 120     ),     list(       field = "species",       headerName = "Species",       align = "center",       headerAlign = "center",       width = 150     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: Create alternating row colors using indexRelativeToCurrentPage:
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head(),   getRowClassName = JS("     function(params) {       return params.indexRelativeToCurrentPage % 2 === 0 ? 'even' : 'odd';     }   "),   sx = list(     "& .even" = list(       backgroundColor = "#f5f5f5"     ),     "& .odd" = list(       backgroundColor = "white"     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
code: DataGrid(   rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, homeworld) |> head(),   getRowClassName = JS("     function(params) {       return params.indexRelativeToCurrentPage % 2 === 0 ? 'even' : 'odd';     }   "),   sx = list(     "& .even" = list(       backgroundColor = "#fafafa",       "&:hover" = list(         backgroundColor = "#eeeeee"       )     ),     "& .odd" = list(       backgroundColor = "white",       "&:hover" = list(         backgroundColor = "#f5f5f5"       )     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: can use ThemeProvider muiMaterial package apply global MUI theme Data Grid. allows customize colors, typography, theme tokens cascade grid automatically.
code: library(shiny) library(muiMaterial) library(muiDataGrid) library(dplyr)  muiMaterialPage(   ThemeProvider(     theme = list(       palette = list(         mode = "dark"       )     ),     DataGrid(       rows = starwars |> select(name, height, mass, species) |> head()     )   ) )

title: Styling Basics
text: ’s comprehensive example combining multiple styling techniques:
code: starwars_styled <- starwars |>   select(name, height, mass, species, homeworld) |>   head()  DataGrid(   rows = starwars_styled,   columns = list(     list(       field = "name",       headerName = "Character",       width = 180,       cellClassName = "bold-cell",       headerAlign = "left"     ),     list(       field = "height",       headerName = "Height (cm)",       width = 120,       type = "number",       align = "right",       headerAlign = "right"     ),     list(       field = "mass",       headerName = "Mass (kg)",       width = 120,       type = "number",       align = "right",       headerAlign = "right"     ),     list(       field = "species",       headerName = "Species",       width = 130,       align = "center",       headerAlign = "center"     ),     list(       field = "homeworld",       headerName = "Homeworld",       width = 140     )   ),   getRowClassName = JS("     function(params) {       const baseClass = params.indexRelativeToCurrentPage % 2 === 0 ? 'even' : 'odd';       if (params.row.species === 'Droid') {         return baseClass + ' droid';       }       return baseClass;     }   "),   sx = list(     border = 2,     borderColor = "primary.light",     borderRadius = 2,     "& .MuiDataGrid-columnHeaders" = list(       backgroundColor = "#1976d2",       color = "darkgreen",       fontSize = 14,       fontWeight = "bold"     ),     "& .bold-cell" = list(       fontWeight = "600"     ),     "& .even" = list(       backgroundColor = "#fafafa"     ),     "& .odd" = list(       backgroundColor = "white"     ),     "& .droid" = list(       backgroundColor = "#e3f2fd !important"     ),     "& .MuiDataGrid-cell:hover" = list(       color = "primary.main"     ),     "& .MuiDataGrid-row:hover" = list(       backgroundColor = "#f5f5f5"     )   ) )

title: Server-Side DataGrid — DataGridServer
text: inputId Character. Shiny input ID. pagination, sorting, filtering changes, new state available input$<inputId> server. value list elements pagination_model (list page pageSize), sort_model (list sort items), filter_model (list items). rows data.frame. Pass full dataset (like DataGrid()) let DataGridServer() handle pagination automatically, pass pre-sliced page together explicit rowCount manual control. columns Column definitions (list lists). NULL, auto-generated names(rows), column's type inferred R class (see DataGrid). rowCount Integer. provided, rows assumed already paginated rowCount used total row count (manual mode). NULL (default), pagination handled automatically full rows dataset. loading Logical. Whether show loading indicator. NULL, MUI defaults FALSE. initialPageSize Integer. Convenience setting initial page size. Builds MUI's initialState prop. NULL, MUI defaults 100. Also sets page size first automatic render grid sent state. Must included pageSizeOptions. pageSizeOptions Integer vector. Available page size options. NULL, MUI defaults c(25, 50, 100). filterDebounce Integer. Milliseconds debounce filter input sending state R. NULL, defaults 300 ms. ... Additional props passed directly MUI DataGrid component. Note: paginationMode, sortingMode, filterMode, pagination set automatically overridden.

title: Server-Side DataGrid — DataGridServer
text: Experimental. DataGridServer() (together processGridParams) specific R package equivalent MUI X Data Grid: MUI ships building blocks (paginationMode = "server" plus callbacks) leaves data layer . wrapper supplies layer R, encodes number opinionated decisions may change future releases. Pin package version rely current behaviour. Decisions worth knowing : Mode selected presence rowCount: supply     pass pre-sliced page (manual mode); omit let full     rows paginated automatically. Changing sort filter resets grid first page. Unrecognised filter operators pass rows warning     (see processGridParams). rows id column, ids generated     positionally stable across sort/filter changes.     Supply stable, unique id column use row selection. initialPageSize, initialState, initial sort     filter seed grid first render. React component     reads mounts, changing reactively afterwards     (e.g. selectInput) effect already-mounted     grid. change page size mount, drive grid's     controls rather re-rendering new initialPageSize.

title: Server-Side DataGrid — DataGridServer
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple usage: pass the full dataset, pagination is handled automatically output$grid <- renderReact({   DataGridServer("grid_params",     rows = my_data,     initialPageSize = 10L,     pageSizeOptions = c(10L, 25L, 50L)   ) })  # Manual usage: handle pagination yourself (e.g. database queries) output$grid <- renderReact({   result <- processGridParams(my_data, input$grid_params, pageSize = 10L)   DataGridServer("grid_params",     rows = result$rows,     rowCount = result$rowCount,     initialPageSize = 10L,     pageSizeOptions = c(10L, 25L, 50L)   ) }) } # }

title: Process server-side grid parameters — processGridParams
text: Experimental. helper specific R package equivalent MUI X Data Grid, leaves server-side data layer entirely developer. reimplements MUI's server-mode filtering sorting semantics R, behaviour may change releases can differ edge cases MUI's client-side filtering. Decisions worth knowing : string filters case-insensitive (except , case-sensitive), missing values always sort last, unrecognised filter operators pass rows warning.

title: muiDataGrid 0.1.2
text: DataGrid() now infers MUI column types R classes (numeric columns become number, logical columns boolean) warns supplied id column contains duplicate values. Added server-side support via DataGridServer() processGridParams(), apply pagination, sorting, filtering R send current page browser. sort/filter pass memoized paginating large dataset longer re-sorts every row page change. DataGridServer() hardened: validates initialPageSize pageSizeOptions, rejects two live outputs sharing one inputId, warns nrow(rows) exceeds manual rowCount, strips protected props (paginationMode, sortingMode, filterMode, pagination). Filter input server grid now debounced independently pagination sorting changes. Bundled MUI X Data Grid locales multi-language support. Require muiMaterial (>= 0.2.0) warn attach incompatible version installed, since grid shares bundled MUI runtime. Documented attribution licensing bundled MUI X Data Grid JavaScript (see LICENSE.note).
